creator: empty.japan
caption:
Six months ago, I moved from Kyoto to a small village on the outskirts of Nagano Prefecture.

Someone left a comment under my recent reel saying, “So what do you even do on weekends there? Visit the same Temple and stare at rice fields?” First of all, what’s wrong with that? 😂 Everyone has their own version of fun! But honestly, I had my doubts too. I spent almost a year deciding where to move next because I wasn’t sure if life in a small village would suit me. Spoiler alert: it totally does, and it’s been beyond anything I could have imagined!

This summer, I’ve tried more amazing things than I have in my entire life - endless hiking trails, onsens, shower climbing, fishing, MTB trails, and even wake surfing on one of the nearby lakes. Oh, and yes, I now have a persimmon tree in my garden, and the joy I get from picking my own fruit is unreal 😂

I can’t wait for winter to finally arrive so I can explore all the ski resorts around my house. Up until now, I’ve only been to Hakuba, Nozawaonsen, and a couple of spots in Hokkaido, and I used to think there weren’t that many other places to check out… but wow, was I wrong. The number of amazing lesser-known ski spots here is insane, and I’ll definitely be sharing my adventures and all the best finds with you!

Of course, I get that if you’re someone who loves city life and isn’t into these activities, this place might feel a bit dull. But for me? I wouldn’t trade it for anything - especially with fiber-optic internet right in the house! (And just to be clear, not every village is like this—believe it or not, I spent almost a year deciding on the perfect place to move 😂)
